paper_title: U-Define: Designing User Workflows for Hard and Soft Constraints in LLM-Based Planning

access.notes: The project is a mutable private OSF node exposed through the paper's anonymous view-only link, not an immutable OSF registration. The API reports read-only anonymous access and no license. Preserve the view-only token because unauthenticated API requests without it return HTTP 401. No redistribution or reuse license was found in the node metadata or source archive.

timing.assessment: The node's reported last modification predates immutable arXiv v1 by about 140 days. This supports pre-v1 availability of the currently exposed files, but the unregistered mutable node and archive's lack of a commit identifier prevent a cryptographic claim that this is an immutable or exact paper-release snapshot.

artifacts.inspection: ZIP integrity passed. It contains 104 files (1,536,187 uncompressed bytes) under VibeSync-baseline, including backend/frontend code, prompts, example plans, logs, tests, generated outputs, IDE/cache files, and no repository-level license. The archive name and internal project name remain VibeSync rather than U-Define, and no commit identifier is supplied.
artifacts.security_boundary: The official archive includes a .env with seven non-empty assignments, including an API-key-named value. Values were not recorded, used, or committed. The archive and extracted tree remain ignored to avoid propagating potential credentials. Reviewers should inspect code through the retained local archive without exposing or executing secret-bearing configuration.

artifacts.osf_name: User Study 1 Protocol and Interview Questions.pdf
User Study 2 Protocol and Interview Questions.pdf

extraction.verification: All five OSF files listed by the API were downloaded and matched the API-provided SHA-256 hashes. All four supplemental PDFs produced non-empty text; first and final content were inspected. The source ZIP passed unzip -t and its inventory was inspected without executing the software.

scope_note: This record establishes source availability and provenance only. It does not claim a deep paper review, reproduce model calls, validate study findings, or establish professional planning validity.
